column4: Entry
Comment to be written.
They are born with a seed implanted on their back that grows large over time. Researchers argued over 6 years on the issue of whether this Pokémon should be classified as an animal or a plant.

Bulbasaur's evolution. The plant on their back has grown to a bud. It absorbs nutrients and blooms as a large flower. When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

Absorbs solar energy as nutrition. When it is catching the sun's rays, it often remains quiet and still, but when it is seeking sunlight, it stays on the move. Their flower wafts a dreamily intoxicating aroma that pacifies those spoiling for a fight. It remains unclear as to what they eat to maintain their bulk of 2 meter height and 100 kilogram weight.
Right from birth a flame burns at the end of their tail. They cannot survive if the flame were to go out. Smokers often keep them in the place of lighters.
They flail with their tail to mow down foes, and then shred enemies with their sharp claws. Their powerful tail can hoise five adults (approximately 300 kilograms).

Capable of flying up to 1,400 meters in altitude, it flies about in search of strong opponents. It breathes intense flames that can melt any material, and it burns hotter if it has experienced harsh battles. Unless provoked, it will never torch a weaker foe.
When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force. It takes time for the shell to form and harden after hatching.
Their profile is cute when they are fighting. They are highly popular as pets. Their fur-covered tail is a symbol of longevity.

A brutal Pokémon with pressurized water jets on its shell. They spray water like cannons, hard enough to pierce thick steel, and are very accurate, capable of striking cans from over 160 feet away. Their bulk makes quick movements impossible, but they have overwhelming power to compensate.

When they shed their green skin to grow, they wrap themselves in a silk and transform into a cocoon. Out of all the Pokémon discovered to date, they are the fastest to evolve. The Tiny Bird Pokémon Pidgey is their arch enemy. If Caterpie even sense the presence of Pidgey, they quickly burrow underground. Those too slow to hide fall prey to Pidgey. While a forest may appear peaceful, there are harsh rules that dictate the survival of the fittest.
Caterpie's evolution. While they are encased in a hard shell, the inside is tender, making them vulnerable to harsh attacks. Worse, being mid-metamorphosis, they are challenged to move quickly.

A week to ten days after evolving, Metapod undergo another evolutionary step and metamorphose into Butterfree. Their wings are covered in water-repellent, powder-like scales that enable them to fly even on rainy days.

Common in not only forests but also grasslands. There is a small, sharp poison stinger of around 5 centimeters in length on the top of their head. When faced with a foe, they wiggle the stinger for intimidation. They favor food like Gingko leaves.

A temporary evolutionary form taken while readying the mature form. In their previous guise as Weedle, they are said to eat many leaves in preparation for assuming this pupal form. They are almost incapable of moving.

Kakuna's evolution. They may appear in swarms. They buzz around at dizzying speed and jab repeatedly with the venomous stinger on their tail. When they grow and learn Twineedle, they become almost untouchable.

They do not like to fight. If surrounded, they scatter sand to blind their foes and make their getaway. They hide among trees and tall grass when feeding on small bugs and the like. While they feast on Caterpie and Weedle, they oddly ignore Venonat.

Pidgey's evolution. They dive from an altitude of 800 meters to capture prey. The claws on their feet are powerfully developed. They have no trouble carrying an Exeggcute - their prey - weighing 25 kilograms to their nest 100 kilometers away.

It races through the skies at Mach-2 speed at 1,200 meters altitude. If they encounter a foe while on the ground, they spread their gorgeous wings widely for intimidation. Their search for food leads them to even the seas where they make easy prey of meaninglessly splashing Magikarp. It goes without saying that they are the evolution of Pidgeotto.

Its incisors are long, sharp, and continue growing until its death. They gnaw on hard objects to keep the ever-growing fangs workably short. They feast on nuts like walnuts and acorns. They also like processed food like cheese and milk. With their strong capacity for survival, they can live in dirty places without concern. Recently, it has become a popular Pokémon.

Rattata's evolution. Their rear feet have three toes with small webs in between for swimming across rivers. In addition to nuts (naturally), they also feed on fish. Its disposition is far more violent than looks would suggest. Don't let your hand get too close to its face, as it could bite your hand clean off.

Compared with Pidgey, they are much more aggressive. They mainly search for prey among tall grass where they can be seen busily flitting about. While they have little power, they learn the rare Mirror Move.
Spearow's evolution. They can soar high all day long on their broad wings. Their powerful beaks let them swallow prey like a Rattata whole.

They flick their tongue about to sense danger in their surroundings. This carnivore swallows all food whole with almost no chewing. They love the eggs of Pidgey and Spearow. Rattata are also preyed upon occassionally. If they swallow a Rattata, they can go without food again for a month. They grow by shedding repeatedly. In the winter, they hibernate in the warm soil of forests. In summer, they are also common sights in grasslands.

Ekans' evolution. Consequently, this Pokémon is also a venomous serpent. The markings on their belly have the appearance of a frightening face, which is different depending on the specimen. Timid foes flee at the sight of such a "face." They are skilled at using Glare that induces paralysis in their foe. If you encounter an Arbok on a path, you should avert your gaze. They are mortal enemies of Raticate. The two Pokémon fight viciously like a Seviper and a Zangoose.

They live in forests where they feed mainly on nuts. Forests in which nests of them live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning. Its nature is to store up electricity in the small pouches on each side of their cheeks that are discharged when endangered. Smart and charming with an affectionate nature, they are the most popular Pokémon among researchers. Pikachu evolve into Raichu, but many trainers prevent evolution because they don't want their Pokémon to change appearance. Its scientific name is Mus pikalus.

Their electric strikes can reach 100,000 volts. Unwittingly touching them can cause even foes several times its size to faint. This Pokémon can learn all Electric-type moves. There have been no reports of Raichu sightings in forests.
They dig and live in burrows. When threatened, they curl up to protect themselves. Besides grasslands, they also apepar in mountainous environments.
Sandshrew's evolution. They scurry about quickly. They are adept at attacking using the pointed spines on their back and sharp claws.

Although small, they have venomous needles with devastating power. The females have a smaller horn than the males. Their breeding period comes every year in spring. At the end of summer they lay two eggs that hatch in two weeks, one male and one female. Baby Nidoran are just 10 centimeters long.
Being female, they are placid in nature. They emit ultrasonic cries that can allure enemies. While they are the evolved form of Nidoran♀, they lose the ability to lay eggs upon evolution.

Nidoqueen's body is encased in extremely hard scales that stand up when agitated. It is adept at sending foes flying with harsh tackles. This Pokémon is at its strongest when it is defending its young. As they grow, they learn powerful moves like Mega Punch and Fire Blast.

They flap their large ears like wings when straining to hear faraway sounds. They extend venomous needles when angered. In their breeding season, their horn glows in seven colors for wooing females. Apparently, the larger the horn, the more appealing to females.
They are easily angered. They flail their well-developed horn to skewer even diamonds. While they are the evolved form of Nidoran♂, unlike Nidorina, they do not lose their breeding ability.

Nidoking's tail packs enormously destructive power. With one swing, it can topple a metal tower. If it binds an enemy, it can snap the victim's spine easily. Be careful with the horn as it contains venom. They appear to shy away from fighting Nidoqueen.

Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers, making it popular as pets. However, it is rare and found only in certain areas, most notably in the caves of Mt. Moon. Clefairy is best at moving the move Metronome which triggers a random move from many choices. Occasionally, they startle themselves by using a powerful move that doesn't suit their cute appearance. They are surprisingly well liked by not only people but even their fellow Pokémon.

Neither Clefairy nor Clefable like squabbling. Perhaps that explains why their hearing is so superbly developed. They can even discern the sound of a pin dropping 1 kilometer away. They immediately flee if they hear danger approaching.

They have six gorgeous tails that increase in number as they grow. It is quite warm and cuddly. Inside Vulpix's body burns a flame that never goes out. During the daytime, when the temperatures rise, this Pokémon releases flames from its mouth to prevent its body from growing too hot.

They are clad in a golden fur and have long nine tails. They are a long-living Pokémon that are said to live for a thousand years. Extremely vindicitive, it is said to lay a 1,000-year long curse if its tail is pulled as a joke. According to an enduring legend, 9 noble saints were united and reincarnated as this Pokémon.

Looking into its cute, round eyes causes it to sing a relaxing melody, inducing its enemies to sleep. It never pauses to breathe when it sings, so if its opponent does not fall asleep, its life is endangered. Belying their adorable appearance, they can learn a diverse array of moves. If they become displeased, they inflate themselves like a balloon.

Its fur is the ultimate in luxuriousness. Sleeping alongside a Wigglytuff is simply divine. If any dust gets in this Pokémon's eyes, it is quickly washed away thanks to a thin layer of tears. While they share many moves with Jigglypuff, they additionally learn Hyper Beam.

They live in murky caves on mountains and similar environments, because they live in perpetual darkness. Their eyes retrogressed and disappeared. They emit ultrasonic cries for flitting around in darkness. Apparently, it is very rare for them to fly outside their caves.
They bite with their sharp fangs and suck 300 milliliters in one chomp. Its own blood type changes to that of its victim. It is particularly active in the pitch black of night.

Also known as Walkimendes. At night, they walk up to 300 meters on their two roots. Being plants, it was a mystery as to why they walked. It was finally determined by a recent study that it was for the survival of the species. They scatter their pollen as they walk.

Their pistil emits a horrifically foul stench that makes foes faint up to 2 kilometers away. However, if this Pokémon is feeling calm and secure, it does not release its usual stinky aroma. The fluid that oozes from its mouth isn't drool. It is a nectar that is used to attract prey.
From their world's-largest petals, they scatter wicked plumes of pollen that induce allergic reactions. Other Pokémon can't get near where Vileplume lives.

Growing from the bug's back are mushrooms called Tochukaso. The mushrooms grow in step with the Pokémon. They live under fallen trees in forests. When they grow to a certain size, they move to grasslands and marshes.

They scatter toxic spores from the mushroom's cap. In some parts of the world, the spores are apparently used as ingredients of herbal medicine. Being Paras' evolution, Parasect was included among the Pokémon of forests. However, they are almost impossible to find in forests.

Their eyes serve as radars that let them stay active in darkness. The eyes also emit beams of light. They have been confirmed to be a Bug type. Although they evolve into Venomoth, its pupal form remains a mystery.

The wings are covered in powdery, toxic scales. With every flap of their wings, they scatter the scales in clouds. They are said to live in Fuchsia City's Safari Zone. This is because they are difficult to find elsewhere due to their small population. Their pupal stage is shrouded in mystery.

They burrow through the ground close to the surface. They leave ridged trails of soil in their wake that betray their location. They may appear in the trail of an Onix, probably seeking its leftovers.
They burrow underground to hide themselves, and then strike the unsuspecting target from a different spot. Some researchers categorize Dugtrio, Dodrio, Exeggcute, and Exeggutor as one genre.

Being nocturnal, they sleep through the day. At night, they prowl their territory with eyes aglow. They are a happily beneficial Pokémon that can earn their trainer pocket change using their Pay Day move.

Fickle and ferocious. Beware if they raise their tail straight. It's a sure sign they are preparing to leap and bite. Due to their gorgeous coat, many women keep them as trophy pets. Wild Persian, however, are known to be truly vicious.

They are always suffering from a headache. If their headache intensifies, they start using mysterious powers. It doesn't recall that it used them after the fact, though. Some people believe that its vacant and unaware appearance is simply a ruse.
Its body is composed of water, and it is an expert swimmer. It can often be spotted gliding gracefully through the water. Unlike Psyduck, it is capable of learning the move "Hyper Beam."

Light on their feet and extremely volatile. They can fly into a rage and rampage uncontrollably. While they are usually in good spirits, their disposition can change instantly at the slightest provocation. Some researchers point to a calcium deficiency.

When Primeape becomes furious, its blood circulation is boosted. In turn, its muscles are made even stronger. However, it also becomes much less intelligent at the same time. They will give chase and will not stop however far one runs. It's all over if you lock eyes with one.

Friendly toward people with a loyal nature. Faced with an enemy, they bark and bite to drive their foe away. Cinnabar Island is famous as a home to many Fire-type Pokémon, and its unique ecosphere is thought to have strongly influenced its native Pokémon, with Growlithe being no exception. Growlithe's evolution is dependent on a stone.

There's so many old tales about them that they're called legendary Pokémon, but there are way more of them around than you'd expect. Sanctified paper cuts imprinted with an Arcanine illustration are considered beneficial for keeping evil at bay. Said to be capable of running over 6,200 miles in 24 hours.
The slick black skin is thin and damp. A portion of their internal organs can be seen as a spiral. They can be categorized as relatives of tadpoles.

Poliwag's evolution. While tadpoles grow into frogs, Poliwag remained in tadpole form and grew muscular to end up as Poliwhirl. While their legs are well developed for life on land, they appear to prefer life in water.

Its highly developed, brawny muscles never grow fatigued. It is so tirelessly strong, that it can swim back and forth across the ocean in multiple styles like the crawl and the butterfly without effort. Even pro athletes are no match for their speed. However, it appears their muscles were developed specifically for swimming as they are not fast at running. While they are Poliwhirl's evolution, they remain in tadpole form, unlike Politoed.

They sleep for 16 hours a day. However, even asleep they use a variety of extrasensory powers. They are rarely encountered due to their small population. Moreover, they will teleport instantly if threatened. As a result, they often escape before they could be captured in a rare encounter. They evolve into Kadabra.

"As a psychic boy awoke one morning, he found himself transformed in his bed into a Kadabra." The crowning of the Kadabra-themed short story "The Transformation" as the 2nd Pokémon Literary Award winner is still fresh in our memory. If Pikachu is the Pokémon idol, Kadabra can be considered a favorite among those in the know.

Disdainful of physical attacks, they adeptly wield extrasensory powers to defeat their foes. While they are Kadabra's evolution, no one had been able to prove it conclusively until Pokémon trading became legalized. In battle, Alakazam remains expressionless throughout.
Being entirely musclebound, they have the power to throw a hundred adult people despite their small, childlike stature.
Machop's evolution. Their powerful body never tires. Diligent and hardworking, they help people with work such as transporting heavy cargo.
Its four ruggedly developed arms have the potential to launch a flurry of 1,000 punches in just two seconds. Its fighting spirit flares up when it faces a tough opponent.
Their bud has the appearance of a face. Because of it, they are said to be a type of the legendary mandrake root. They feed mainly on small insects.
It lives by absorbing nutrients in the ground through its root-like feet. The leaves are cutters for shredding foes. They spit a liquid that dissolves anything.
Prey are drawn with a honey-like aroma into Victreebel's mouth where they are melted by a dissolving fluid. This Pokémon is omnivorous and will eat anything that fits in their mouth.
From their crystal-clear eyes, they emit mysterious rays of light. They feed on plankton. 99% of their body is composed of water.

The eighty tentacles move about freely to ensnare prey like small fish. The tentacles also have venomous needles that induce poisoning and full-body paralysis. The large red orbs on its head glow before lashing the vicinity with a harsh ultrasonic blast. This Pokémon's outburst creates rough waves around it, making all fish Pokémon disappear from the surrounding sea. For those qualities, they are called "gangsters of the sea." The longer one has been alive, the fewer tentacles it will have.

A docile Pokémon that does not harm people. That said, they can lose their temper if they are accidentally stepped on in caves, for example. They can be found while hiking on mountains. Being round and easy to hold, people toss them back and forth in Geodude fights.
When walking on sloped mountain trails, Graveler may come tumbling along. While their face wears a smile, their eyes never smile. It is very creepy to see them come rolling with that expression.

They tumble on sloped trails at twice the speed of a Graveler. They are encased in a hard shell composed of sheet rock-like slabs. They grow larger by shedding their shell once a year. With luck, people may find a Golem's discarded shell on a mountain in summer.
Their light body is supported by incredibly strong legs. They can clear a tower in one jump. While this herbivore is peaceful, care must be taken when shoeing them - they can kick.
Ponyta's evolution. They can run up to 240 KMH. They can keep pace with a bullet train while enveloping themselves in flames. They obey people dutifully.

Always vacant and "out to lunch," it is impossible to determine what they are thinking. They live by the water's edge where they deftly catch prey with their tail. Although they have enough smarts to fish that way, they exhibit no other behavior that would indicate any greater intelligence. They frequently swim undersea in search of prey, but are often mistakenly caught themselves.

When Slowpoke went foraging in the sea, they were bitten on their tail by Shellder. This resulted in their evolution into Slowbro. It is unknown as to why they did not try to remove the Shellder. It is suspected that doing so would have required too much effort. They are naturally averse to fighting, and like Slowpoke, appear to prefer zoning out.

They move around while levitating. The units on their sides radiate energy like electromagnetic waves. Although they are fundamentally different from the other Pokémon of the skies, they used to be classified as such because there was no other grouping for them.

Magnemite's evolution. What keeps this Pokémon levitating was unknown until recently, when Bill Sonezaki discovered that its due to magnetic fields. Formed by multiple Magnemite linked together, they discharge intense magnetic rays and high-voltage power.

They always carry a plant stalk for crafting their nest when walking. A recent study also revealed the stalk to be food for emergencies. Several years ago, the mass media reported on the deliciousness of cooking Farfetch'd with their stalk. This news spread worldwide, resulting in a massive decline in their population. In order to prevent their extinction, more people have made an effort to breed these Pokémon.

A two-headed Pokémon discovered thirty years ago. They are probably a mutation. They appear to dislike flying. They seem to be much better at running based on their 100 km/h speed. In the near future, it is feared their wings will devolve and rob them of their ability to fly.

Dodrio's evolution. A rare species that is hardly ever found in the wild. Their three heads express the emotions joy, sadness, and anger. Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. It also has three sets of hearts and lungs as well, so it is capable of running long distances without rest. Some historians have sworn that in ancient times there was a religion that thrived deep in the southern reaches of the globe which revered Dodrio as their symbol.

The light blue-haired hide is thick and tough. They remain active even in intense cold of -40° C. They live in sea caves and the like. During the day, they often drift in the sea, probably searching for food.

Seel's evolution. They are cloaked entirely in pure-white hair. Their blubber reaches an incredible thickness of 30 centimeters. Stores thermal energy in its body, making them highly resistant to cold. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters, searching for Pokémon that will become its prey.

X-rays from the moon transformed toxic sludge into Grimer. They love to eat filthy things. When first discovered, their appearances were limited to empty lots behind factories and so on. However, they have become progressively bolder in the places they appear. Its scientific name is Toxodictyostelium putridum.

They usually keep themselves hidden by mingling with soil. Contact results in a severe poisoning. Analysis of their cells revealed the presence of substances that are inconceivable in living things. It should be no surprise that they are Grimer's evolution.
Encased within a shell that is harder than diamond. The body inside, however, is surprisngly tender. They are good with the move Clamp which squeezes the victim between the shell halves.

Said to be Shellder' only evolution. The shell is extremely durable. Even a direct hit from a napalm bomb can't destroy it. The shell opens only when attacking. Their Spike Cannon - which repeatedly stabs the foe with spikes - is devastating.

A life form composed of a diffuse, gaseous matter. Even a much larger foe would fall in two seconds if enveloped in their gas. Many mysteries surround this Pokémon. Some theories claim they are photochemical smog come to life while others claim they are extra-dimensional beings. The answer remains unknown.

Gastly's evolution. They learn moves like Dream Eater and Psychic. It is said that if you feel as though you are being watched in a dark place with no one else around, there is a Haunter lurking. There is a Kantonian folktale that an ancient giant Haunter terrorized people and killed many by eating their souls.

Always thought (and confirmed 30 years ago) to be Haunter's evolution. Many Gengar are playful and are often the culprits behind shadows that laugh in the moonlight. Yet old folk stories say that it emerges from darkness to steal the lives of those who become lost in mountains. Coincidentally, mountain shrines honoring Gengar exist.

They usually live submerged in soil. They burrow at 80 KMH in search of prey. Like other Ground-type Pokémon, Onix is strong against Poison-type attacks. Conversely, they are vulnerable to Water-type attacks.

Rumored to be the descendant of the legendary creature, Baku, which is said to have eaten dreams. Some even suggest that the myth of Baku actually referred to a Drowzee. It is famous as the first Pokémon to use a move combo - Hypnosis followed by Dream Eater. They are gluttonous herbivores. From their four stomachs, they repeatedly bring up eaten food and rechew it to feed. They are not popular among women due to their appearance. They also have a habit of muttering under their breath to themselves.

Drowzee's evolution. They carry around a pendulum-like object. They are skilled at using Psychic. There once was an incident in which it took away a child it hypnotized. On the other hand, there are some Hypno that assist doctors with patients who can't sleep at night in hospitals.
Found near the sea. When they fight, they raise their pincers to intimidate their foe. However, they usually prefer to avoid fighting. If a pincer is torn off, it grows back.
The hard pincers have 10,000-horsepower strength. However, they are also too large, making this Pokémon ponderous in motion. They may also be seen deep inside caves.

A completely enigmatic creature. They generate an unpleasant buzzing noise. While many live in power plants, etc., they appear to have spread in towns in recent years by traveling on power lines. They possess Electric-type moves. They are reckless and prone to exploding without warning, making it difficult for researchers to study their relation to modern Poké Balls.
Voltorb's evolution. Because they explode at the slightest provocation, they are feared with the nickname "Bomb Ball".

While they appear to be eggs of some sort, recent studies identified them to be creatures similar to plant seeds. While some researchers claim that they are the same as the plant growing out of Bulbasaur's back, that theory is yet to be proven. Its scientific name is Cocos ovula.

Also known as the walking tropical rainforest. Each of their fruits has a face with its own unique will. However, they are friendly and never appear to squabble. Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

According to folklore, the life of a Cubone is a life of tragedy. The headpiece it wears is the skull of its dead mother and the only memento it has left of her. It pines for the mother it will never see again. The stains on the skull it wears are made by the tears it loudly sheds when they feel lonely. It never removes its skull helmet, so no one has ever seen this Pokémon's real face.
The evolved form of a Cubone that has grown tough by overcoming the grief of losing its mother. Originally small and very weak, it turned ferocious and deadly when it began using bones.
Their legs can be freely extended and contracted. Kicks can be delivered to even faraway foes. They are surpsingly docile when they are not fighting. Also known as 'The Demon Of Kickboxing.'

A Pokémon supposedly inhabited by the spirit of a pro boxer. They throw punches faster than a bullet train. Although their habitat is unknown, based on their behavior, they have been categorized as a grassland Pokémon, the same as Hitmonlee.

At 2.5 meters, their tongue is twice as long as their height. The tongue is usually stowed and curled up in their mouth. When hunting or attacking, they can move their tongue as if it were an arm. A ravenous appetite and a lazy nature must have shaped this Pokémon's evolution.

The thin-skinned, balloon-like body is filled with a powerfully toxic gas. Getting too close to this foul-smelling Pokémon makes eyes flood with tears. First discovered in a munitions factory, their habitat has been growing in recent years.
While very rare, mutations can result in Koffing twins that are joined together. There is a lab developing a medicine for bleaching the toxicity out of Weezing.

While they lack intelligence, they have plenty of power. Their ramming can shatter even building towers. Though they were thought to not be suited as house pets, they have been tamed to be surprisingly docile in the Kalos region, where Rhyhorn racing is a popular sport.

Revered since ancient times as a mighty warrior and quite possibly the first Pokémon ever tamed. Protected by an armor-like hide, it can survive in lava over 3,600 degrees. Evolving from Rhyhorn made this Pokémon stand on their hind legs. This process prompts development of their brain and intelligence. Its horn can bore holes through boulders and even uncut diamonds.

They lay several eggs a day. The eggs are nutritionally rich and fantastically delicious. They are peaceful and do not like to fight much, so they are often seen assisting nurses at Pokémon Centers. A wild Chansey is rare and elusive, so anyone who finds one must be lucky indeed.

A plant-type Pokémon covered in blue plant vines that hide their actual form. They try to entangle anyone approaching too closely. Many researchers have attempted to cut away all the vines in hopes of seeing what they hide, but all attempts ended in failure due to Tangela's furious resistance. Its scientific name is Pseudohedera vita.

The females place and raise their young in belly pouches for around three years. Kangaskhan's maternal love is so deep that it will brave death to protect its offspring. They are skilled at pummeling with Comet Punch.

They keep balance using their spirally coiled tail. When they sense danger, they may spit black ink. Every spring, the females each lay several thousand eggs on seaweed, but most end up as Goldeen bait. Rare among Pokémon, the males raise its young.
Horsea's evolution. Their nature turns vicious from evolving. Carelessly trying to touch them can result in punctures from the spikes growing all over their body. The intense pain can cause fainting.

The dorsal and ventral fins are built up like muscles. They help propel this Pokémon through water at a speed of 5 knots. Having little intelligence, they are known to unwittingly swim into a Gyarados' mouth where they become an easy meal.
Goldeen's evolution. Using their drill-like horn, they hollow out boulders to make their nest. They swim up rivers to spawn every year.

They are commonly found on beaches. At night, their central core flashes brightly. They are gynandrous; they possess both male and female reproductive organs. If a part of their body is cut off, they regenerate the missing body parts.

They use advanced moves like Confusion, Light Screen, Meditate, and Substitute. Being skilled mimes, they are masters at convincingly tricking people. It is said the walls they mine turn real. Their fingertips end in suction pads with which they apparently climb the walls they create. Little headway has been made in their study owing to the scarcity of specimens.
Scyther is blindingly fast. Its blazing speed enhances the lethality of the twin scythes on its forearms. They are relatively rare with few sightings in the wild, possibly due to its speed.

An extremely rare Pokémon. They become volcanically enraged if any attempt is made to remove the clothlike shroud around their waist. That reaction informs us they have a sense of shame. While they speak in a humanlike manner, their language is indecipherable. Linguistic research is ongoing.

They love to feast on powerful electricity. They appear somewhat frequently at major power plants. Like Scyther, they are captured relatively rarely. Both Scyther and Electabuzz can distinguish colors. They have a natural affinity for the color red in particular.

A species found near a volcano's crater. They breathe fire. Their body temperature can get extremely high, a few specimens even being recorded at 1,200 degrees Celsius. This Pokémon is very hard to spot in the wild with only a few captured.

The two long horns are powerful. Once they clamp tight on their prey, they won't loosen until the victim is torn apart. They are extremely rare Pokémon with very few reported wild sightings. They appear to prefer the color green.
When ramming, they whip their body harshly with their three tails. They will not attack unless they are attacked first. They can be found in places like the Fuschia City Safari Zone.

It is virtually worthless in terms of both power and speed. If anything were to happen, they immediately use Splash and flop about. It is the most weak and pathetic Pokémon in the world… Or is it? Records exist of Magikarp jumping incredible distances, and some recent studies made an incredible discovery - dragon cells were found in Magikarp's body. Anticipation runs high for deeper studies, with some theorizing that research may provide answers for Gyarados' Mega Evolution.

Extremely vicious and brutal. They completely incinerate all matter with Hyper Beam fired from their mouth. Apparently it can live as deep as the seafloor or as high as above the surface. The first recorded specimen in modern times was a corpse that washed up on shore 35 years ago. They are omnivorous with fangs capable of crushing boulders. Their steel-hard scales can be fashioned into jewelry like rings. Such accessories command astonishing prices.

Being placid in nature, they prefer to ferry people across water on their back rather than fight. Needless to say, they are skilled at using the move Surf. They can also learn a diverse mix of moves like Mist, Confuse Ray, and Hydro Pump. Once driven almost to the point of extinction, it would sing plaintively as it sought what few others of its kind still remain. Following protective regulations, though, there is now an overabundance of them.

Its scientific name is Amorphozodium amorphum. It can freely recombine its own cellular structure to transform into other life-forms, though if it relies on its memory, it tends to fail. They learn almost no moves of their own. Ditto can be considered one of the most enigmatic Pokémon ever, some even going so far as to say that it possibly has a connection to Mew.
Eevee has an unstable genetic makeup that suddenly mutates due to the environment in which it lives. The question of why only Eevee has such unstable genes has still not been solved.
Their body's cellular structure is close to that of water molecules. As a result, they can melt away invisibly in water.
A sensitive Pokémon that easily becomes sad or angry. It accumulates negative ions in the atmosphere to blast out lightning bolts sometimes reaching 10000-volts.
They have a flame sac inside their body. They inhale deeply, and then blow flames, sometimes of 1,700 degrees Celsius.

Roughly 20 years ago, Silph Co., which undertakes R&D into Pokémon and related products, successfully applied state-of-the-art technology to create this manmade Pokémon - the world's first. While they are exceedingly rare, they somehow came to be offered as prizes by the Game Corner in Celadon City. Investigations by the authorities on the matter, assisted by a former Indigo League Champion, found that Team Rocket had a hand in this.
A creature somewhere between sea snails and squids that lived in ancient seas. They swam by waving their ten feet. They fed on plankton and small fish.

Thought to be Omanyte's evolution. They grew to around 1 meter height. They preyed on fish up to around 50 centimeters. They hunted by grabbing and clinging to prey with their dexterously developed tentalces and biting at the same time.

Said to be the ancestor of both hermit crabs and of turtles. They kept themselves sheltered in their tough shell. They appear to have disliked fighting. They are thought to have been good swimmers, although not especially fast. Recently, a Kabuto fossil was found in a mountain cave 1,000 meters above sea level. Its discovery revealed that the area had once been submerged under the sea.

Kabuto's evolution. It swims expertly underwater where they hunted. They captured prey with their sharp scythe arms, tearing at foes with sickle-like forelegs and draining the them of their body fluids entirely. Evolution apparently flipped their nature from timid to aggressive. The relationship of Kabuto and Kabutops can be likened to that of Mogwai and Gremlins. Its scientific name is Kabutops maximus.

One of the contenders for the progenitor of birds. They flew on broad wings while emitting high-pitched cries. From the skull's structure, it is evident their brain was relatively large. Some biologists claim they communicated by crying. They must have caught small creatures with their large claws and preyed on them, using their stiff, sawlike fangs to shred victims into pieces. A very recent discovery of their fossilized droppings attest to this theory.

Among the heaviest of all Pokémon, they are unsatisfied unless they eat 400 kilograms of food a day. As soon as they finish eating, they fall asleep. While they usually live in mountains, they occassionally descend and appear in towns. They are gluttonous and will consume even moldy food without ever getting sick.

They fly among wintry mountains with their long, flowing tails streaming in their wake. Folklore has it that those seeing in Articuno in flight become so enraptured by its beauty that they die rooted to the spot. The magnificent, seemingly translucent wings of this legendary bird Pokémon are said to be made of ice. It chills moisture in the atmosphere to create snow while flying.

A legendary bird Pokémon that appears with a gigantic thunder storm. It flies with the buzz and crack of sparking electricity. It gains power if it is stricken by lightning bolts. Known in legend as the divine bird presiding over the heavens, it's said that Zapdos spend thousands of years above the clouds. Perhaps lightning is their way of showing anger.

Known as the legendary bird of fire. "The Phoenix" - the famous legend of a bird whose fresh blood confers immortality when drunk - is said to be modeled after Moltres, though others believe that it refers to Ho-Oh. Those who see it are overwhelmed by its flaming orange wings. If injured, it dips its body in the molten magma of a volcano to burn and heal itself.

Even when young, Dratini reach a length of nearly 2 meters. They grow by repeatedly shedding. Originally thought to be a mythical creature, it was first discovered when a Safari Zone visitor caught one in the park's pond. The lucky angler became an overnight sensation with news of the discovery exploding, sporting headlines like "Legendary Pokémon Live!" This fortuitous accident convinced many that all legendary Pokémon exist without any doubt, igniting a massive wave of Pokémon expeditions.

A mystical Pokémon that exudes a gentle aura. Has the ability to change climate conditions by discharging energy from the crystals on its neck and tail. They live in the seas and lakes. They can freely summon rain and snow. Though they have no wings, they are said to fly high in the sky.

In spite of their bulky appearance, it is said to be capable of flying around the globe in about 16 hours. Incur the wrath of this normally kind Pokémon, and it will smash everything to smithereens before it's satisfied. Due to this, many legends paint Dragonite as The God of Destruction.

There is an urban legend of a mad scientist who conducted horrific gene splicing and DNA engineering experiments to create a terrifyingly brutal creature. Could such a frightening Pokémon really exist?

Able to transform into any Pokémon and use an incredible variety of moves, Mew is said to possess the genetic composition of all Pokémon. For that reason, it is sometimes referred to as the "Ancestor of all Pokémon." It's so rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts, some even claiming that the species is extinct. Apparently, it appears only to the pure-hearted and strong-willed.
